name: Bulk RNA-seq Analysis
description: Differential expression analysis from RNA-seq count data
prompt: Analyze the uploaded RNA-seq count matrix (genes x samples, raw integer counts) with a documented sample/condition design. First record the organism, genome build and annotation version (e.g. GRCh38, Ensembl/GENCODE release) used to generate counts. (1) QC: inspect library sizes, per-sample count distributions, and a PCA/sample-correlation plot to spot outliers and batch effects; filter low-count genes (e.g. keep genes with >=10 counts in >=N samples). (2) Normalize and run differential expression with pydeseq2 (DESeq2 model), including batch/covariates in the design if present. (3) Test {comparison}, applying Benjamini-Hochberg FDR; report log2FC and adjusted p-values, flagging at FDR<0.05. (4) Volcano and MA plots. (5) GO/KEGG/Reactome enrichment via gget/Enrichr with FDR correction. (6) Heatmap of top 50 DE genes (z-scored). Validate direction against known marker genes; note replicate count and power limits. Set a random seed and record package versions. Deliverables: save figures as PNG, the full DE table and enrichment tables as CSV, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat and name every file. If the comparison groups, design, or organism/build are ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

placeholders.label: Groups to compare (e.g., treatment vs control)

name: Single-Cell RNA-seq Pipeline
description: Full scRNA-seq analysis from raw counts to cell types
prompt: Run a complete single-cell RNA-seq pipeline (scanpy/anndata) on the uploaded count matrix. Record organism, reference genome build and annotation version, and set scanpy/numpy random seeds for reproducibility. (1) QC: filter cells and genes on mitochondrial %, gene counts, and total UMIs using distribution-informed thresholds; flag and optionally remove doublets (e.g. Scrublet). (2) Normalize to counts-per-cell and log1p-transform. (3) Select highly variable genes. (4) Scale, run PCA, and assess batch effects across samples; apply batch correction (Harmony/BBKNN/scanorama) only if needed. (5) Build the neighbor graph and compute UMAP. (6) Leiden clustering. (7) Rank marker genes per cluster (Wilcoxon, BH-adjusted). (8) Annotate cell types using canonical markers for {tissue}, validating against expected populations and flagging ambiguous clusters. Record package and reference versions. Deliverables: save QC/UMAP/marker figures as PNG, marker and cluster-composition tables as CSV, and the annotated AnnData object as .h5ad, plus a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat and name the files. If tissue or expected populations are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions and note limitations.

name: Variant Calling & Annotation
description: Identify and annotate genetic variants from sequencing data
prompt: Process the uploaded sequencing data (FASTQ/BAM) for variant calling against {reference}; record the exact reference build, source, and annotation versions used. (1) Read QC (FastQC-style metrics), then align reads (BWA-MEM/minimap2), mark duplicates, and report mapping rate, coverage and duplication using samtools/pysam. (2) Call SNPs and indels (e.g. GATK HaplotypeCaller/bcftools) with base recalibration where appropriate. (3) Filter variants by quality metrics (QUAL, DP, GQ) and standard hard-filter or VQSR thresholds; report Ts/Tv as a sanity check. (4) Annotate variants with gene names, functional impact, and population frequencies from gnomAD (note version). (5) Flag clinically relevant variants using ClinVar (note release date). (6) Generate a summary report. Record tool/package versions and any seeds. Deliverables: save the filtered annotated VCF, a variant summary table as CSV, QC/coverage figures as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat and name the files, noting limitations. If the reference build, data type, or sample design is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: GWAS Analysis
description: Genome-wide association study analysis pipeline
prompt: Perform a genome-wide association study on the uploaded genotype and phenotype data; record the genome build, variant annotation source, and phenotype definition. (1) Sample/variant QC: filter by MAF > {maf} (default 0.01 if unset), variant and sample call rate, HWE in controls, heterozygosity outliers, and relatedness/duplicate checks. (2) Population stratification via PCA; retain top PCs as covariates and inspect for structure. (3) Run association tests (logistic for binary, linear for quantitative traits) adjusting for PCs and relevant covariates; report the genomic inflation factor (lambda). (4) Generate Manhattan and QQ plots (matplotlib). (5) Identify genome-wide significant loci (p < 5e-8) and define independent signals via clumping/LD. (6) Annotate lead SNPs with nearby genes and known GWAS Catalog associations. Set seeds and record tool/reference versions. Deliverables: save the full summary statistics and significant-loci tables as CSV, plots as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note power/ancestry limitations. If the phenotype, build, or covariates are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: ChIP-seq Peak Calling
description: Identify transcription factor binding sites from ChIP-seq
prompt: Analyze the uploaded ChIP-seq data for {factor}; record the organism, genome build and annotation version, and confirm an input/IgG control is available. (1) Read QC (quality, adapter content), align (BWA/Bowtie2), mark duplicates, and report mapping rate, duplication, library complexity, and cross-correlation/FRiP enrichment metrics. (2) Call peaks against the matched control with appropriate parameters for {peak_type} peaks (e.g. MACS2 narrow vs broad), using a defined q-value threshold and assessing reproducibility across replicates (IDR if available). (3) Annotate peaks relative to genomic features (promoters, enhancers, gene bodies). (4) Run motif enrichment (e.g. HOMER/MEME) and check the expected {factor} motif as a positive control. (5) Generate normalized coverage tracks (deeptools) and peak visualizations. (6) Identify putative target genes. Record tool/reference versions and any seeds. Deliverables: save peak BED/annotation tables and target-gene lists as CSV, figures/tracks as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and flag confounds (input quality, blacklist regions). If genome build or control availability is unclear, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

placeholders.label: Peak type (narrow for TFs, broad for histone marks)

name: ATAC-seq Chromatin Accessibility
description: Map open chromatin regions from ATAC-seq data
prompt: Analyze the uploaded ATAC-seq data; record organism, genome build and annotation version. (1) Read QC and alignment (Bowtie2), then ATAC-specific QC: fragment-size distribution (nucleosome periodicity), TSS enrichment score, and FRiP; remove mitochondrial and duplicate reads and apply Tn5 shift. (2) Call accessibility peaks (MACS2) against a defined threshold, assessing replicate reproducibility. (3) Differential accessibility between {comparison} with a count-based model (DESeq2/edgeR) and BH FDR correction (flag at FDR<0.05). (4) Footprinting for transcription factor binding (e.g. TOBIAS/HINT), validating against expected motifs. (5) Annotate peaks with genomic features and nearby genes. (6) Integrate with gene expression data if provided. (7) Visualize normalized genome-browser tracks (deeptools). Set seeds and record tool/reference versions. Deliverables: save peak and differential-accessibility tables as CSV, figures/tracks as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note confounds and limitations. If the comparison groups or build are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: DNA Methylation Analysis
description: Analyze methylation array or bisulfite sequencing data
prompt: Analyze the uploaded DNA methylation data ({data_type}); record organism, genome build, and array manifest or aligner version. (1) QC and normalization appropriate to the platform (array: detection p-value filtering, probe/sample QC, dye-bias and functional/quantile normalization, drop SNP/cross-reactive probes; bisulfite: alignment, coverage filtering); report beta/M-value distributions. (2) Identify differentially methylated positions (DMPs) and regions (DMRs) between {comparison} using established methods (limma/minfi or methylKit/DMRcate) with BH FDR correction; check for cell-composition and batch confounders and adjust as needed. (3) Annotate DMRs with genes and CpG islands/shores. (4) Pathway enrichment on associated genes with FDR correction. (5) Visualize methylation patterns across key regions (heatmaps, region tracks). (6) Correlate with gene expression if available. Set seeds and record tool/reference versions. Deliverables: save DMP/DMR tables and enrichment results as CSV, figures as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations. If platform, build, or comparison is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

placeholders.label: Data type (450K array, EPIC array, WGBS, RRBS)

name: Gene Set Enrichment Analysis
description: GSEA and pathway enrichment from gene lists or ranked data
prompt: Perform gene set enrichment on the uploaded gene list or ranked gene data. First record the organism and gene-ID type (symbol/Ensembl/Entrez), mapping IDs consistently, and confirm an appropriate background gene set. (1) Run over-representation analysis (ORA) against GO, KEGG, and Reactome using a defined universe (gget/gseapy/Enrichr). (2) If fold-changes are provided, run pre-ranked GSEA on the ranked list with a fixed seed and adequate permutations. (3) Report enriched pathways with FDR-adjusted p-values (BH) and normalized enrichment scores, flagging at FDR<0.05. (4) Create dot plots and an enrichment map of significant terms. (5) Identify key driver/leading-edge genes. (6) Summarize coherent biological themes, noting redundancy among overlapping terms. Record database release versions and package versions. Deliverables: save enrichment result tables as CSV, plots as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations (background choice, gene-set staleness). If organism or ID type is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: Gene Regulatory Network
description: Infer and visualize transcriptional regulatory networks
prompt: Infer a gene regulatory network from the uploaded expression data for {organism}; record the organism, gene-ID type, and expression units (set numpy random seed for reproducibility). (1) Preprocess expression (filtering, normalization) and select candidate regulators using a curated transcription-factor list for {organism}. (2) Apply network inference with arboreto (GRNBoost2 or GENIE3), running multiple seeds to assess stability. (3) Score and threshold regulatory interactions (importance cutoff or top-N edges), reporting the chosen criterion. (4) Identify key hub regulators via network centrality (networkx). (5) Validate predicted edges against known TF-target interactions from databases/literature (e.g. via gget) as a positive control. (6) Visualize the top regulatory subnetwork. Record tool/package and reference TF-list versions. Deliverables: save the edge list and hub/centrality tables as CSV, the network figure as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations (correlation vs causation, sample-size dependence). If organism or expression units are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: Genome Annotation
description: Annotate genes, repeats, and features in a genome assembly
prompt: Annotate the uploaded genome assembly; first record the organism, assembly version, and the reference databases/lineage used (set seeds where tools allow). (1) Assess assembly quality first (contiguity, base composition) and soft-mask repeats before gene prediction. (2) Predict protein-coding genes with ab initio and homology/evidence-based methods (e.g. AUGUSTUS plus protein/transcript evidence). (3) Identify non-coding RNAs (tRNA via tRNAscan-SE, rRNA, miRNA). (4) Annotate repetitive elements (RepeatMasker/RepeatModeler). (5) Functional annotation via InterPro/Pfam domains and GO terms (biopython/gget). (6) Generate GFF3/GTF annotation files and compute assembly/annotation statistics (N50, gene count, BUSCO completeness against the appropriate lineage). Validate gene models against conserved single-copy orthologs as a sanity check. Record tool and database versions. Deliverables: save GFF3/GTF annotation files, a feature-summary table as CSV, plots (e.g. BUSCO, feature counts) as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations. If organism or BUSCO lineage is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: Comparative Genomics
description: Compare genomes across species for evolutionary insights
prompt: Perform comparative genomics across {species}; record each species' genome build/annotation version and data sources (set seeds where applicable). (1) Identify orthologous genes using a reciprocal-best-hit or OrthoFinder-style approach (biopython/gget). (2) For one-to-one orthologs, build codon-aware alignments and compute dN/dS ratios to detect positive selection, applying multiple-testing correction across genes. (3) Identify lineage-specific gene gains and losses from orthogroup presence/absence. (4) Analyze synteny blocks between assemblies. (5) Build a phylogenetic tree from concatenated conserved single-copy orthologs with branch support (etetoolkit/phylogenetics), checking topology against the accepted species tree as a sanity check. (6) Identify rapidly evolving or expanded gene families. Validate selected positive-selection hits against literature. Record tool/reference versions. Deliverables: save ortholog, dN/dS, and gene-family tables as CSV, tree and synteny figures as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations (annotation quality, alignment sensitivity). If the species set or builds are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: Metagenomics Analysis
description: Taxonomic and functional profiling of microbial communities
prompt: Analyze the uploaded metagenomic sequencing data; record the sequencing type (16S vs shotgun), reference database, and version (set seeds for any randomized steps). (1) Read QC, adapter/quality trimming, and host read removal against the appropriate host genome; report retained read counts. (2) Taxonomic classification and profiling (e.g. Kraken2/MetaPhlAn or amplicon ASVs) and build a feature table. (3) Compute alpha diversity (Shannon, Chao1) and beta diversity (Bray-Curtis, UniFrac) with appropriate rarefaction/normalization (scikit-bio). (4) Differential abundance testing between {comparison} using compositionally aware methods (e.g. ANCOM-BC/ALDEx2) with BH FDR correction, flagging at FDR<0.05. (5) Functional profiling (KEGG pathways, CAZymes). (6) Visualize community composition (stacked bars), ordination (PCoA), and diversity. Validate with expected taxa and include negative/positive controls if present. Record tool/database versions. Deliverables: save abundance, diversity, and differential-abundance tables as CSV, figures as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note compositional and depth confounds. If data type, host, or comparison is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: RNA Velocity Analysis
description: Infer cell state dynamics from spliced/unspliced RNA
prompt: Perform RNA velocity analysis on the uploaded single-cell data (scvelo/scanpy/anndata); record the organism and reference build, and set random seeds for reproducibility. (1) Quantify or load spliced and unspliced counts and verify per-cell spliced/unspliced proportions are reasonable (flag if loom/velocyto matrices are missing). (2) Preprocess (filter, normalize, select HVGs, compute moments) and fit RNA velocity models (stochastic and/or dynamical), reporting which model and parameters. (3) Project velocity vectors onto the existing UMAP embedding. (4) Identify root and terminal cell states (velocity pseudotime/terminal-state inference). (5) Compute latent time. (6) Identify and rank driver genes for each lineage transition, validating top drivers against known lineage markers as a sanity check, and flag confounds (cell-cycle, ambient RNA, low unspliced fractions). Record package and reference versions. Deliverables: save velocity/UMAP and latent-time figures as PNG, driver-gene tables as CSV, the processed AnnData object as .h5ad, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations. If organism or expected lineages are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: Spatial Transcriptomics
description: Analyze spatially resolved gene expression data
prompt: Analyze the uploaded spatial transcriptomics data ({platform}) with scanpy/anndata; record organism, reference build, and whether data is spot-based (Visium) or single-cell-resolution (MERFISH/Slide-seq/Xenium), setting random seeds. (1) Platform-appropriate QC (counts/genes per spot or cell, negative-probe/control checks for imaging platforms) and normalization (log-normalize or SCTransform-equivalent). (2) Identify spatially variable genes (e.g. Moran's I / squidpy). (3) Cluster spots/cells and annotate tissue regions, validating with canonical markers. (4) Map cell types using reference scRNA-seq via deconvolution (spot-based) or label transfer (single-cell-resolution) if available. (5) Analyze spatial co-localization and neighborhood/communication patterns. (6) Visualize key gene expression and cluster labels overlaid on tissue coordinates/images. Record package and reference versions. Deliverables: save spatial and QC figures as PNG, spatially-variable-gene and region tables as CSV, the processed AnnData object as .h5ad, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations (resolution, segmentation, reference match). If the platform specifics or reference availability are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: Variant Annotation & Interpretation
description: Annotate and prioritize genetic variants for clinical relevance
prompt: Annotate and interpret the uploaded variant list (VCF or table) in the context of {phenotype}; first record the genome build, transcript set, and database versions, and normalize/left-align variants to that build. (1) Add functional annotations (missense, nonsense, splice-site, frameshift) with consequence on canonical transcripts. (2) Query population frequency from gnomAD (note version) and flag rare/novel variants. (3) Check ClinVar for known pathogenic/likely-pathogenic variants (note release). (4) Add in-silico impact predictions (CADD, PolyPhen, SIFT) and note where they disagree. (5) Flag variants in ACMG-actionable genes. (6) Prioritize variants relevant to {phenotype}, applying ACMG/AMP-style evidence reasoning and flagging quality/coverage confounds. Record database and tool versions. Deliverables: save the annotated, prioritized variant table as CSV, any summary figures as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report with interpretation caveats; summarize in chat, name the files, and note that computational predictions require clinical confirmation. If the genome build or phenotype scope is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

placeholders.label: Clinical phenotype or disease of interest

name: Phylogenetic Analysis
description: Build and visualize phylogenetic trees from sequences
prompt: Perform a phylogenetic analysis on the uploaded sequences; first record sequence type (nucleotide vs protein), count, and any outgroup, and set a random seed. (1) Inspect sequences for length/quality outliers, then align with an appropriate method (MAFFT/MUSCLE) and trim poorly aligned columns. (2) Select the best-fitting evolutionary model (e.g. ModelFinder/jModelTest) and report it. (3) Build the tree (maximum-likelihood via IQ-TREE/RAxML, or Bayesian) with biopython/etetoolkit. (4) Assess branch support via bootstrap (>=1000 replicates) or posterior probabilities, noting weakly supported nodes. (5) Root appropriately, visualize, and annotate the tree. Sanity-check the topology against expected/known relationships and flag long-branch-attraction risk. Record tool and model versions and the seed. Deliverables: save the alignment and tree files (FASTA/Newick), a support/branch-length summary table as CSV, the annotated tree figure as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and interpret the key evolutionary relationships with their support and limitations.

name: Gene Expression Atlas
description: Query and visualize gene expression across tissues and conditions
prompt: Create a gene expression atlas for: {genes}; first map each symbol to stable identifiers, note the organism and reference annotation version, and flag any unresolved symbols. (1) Query GTEx for tissue-level expression patterns (note GTEx release) using gget/database lookups. (2) Query GEO/ArrayExpress for relevant condition-specific datasets, recording accession IDs. (3) Generate tissue expression heatmaps (log-scaled, z-scored where appropriate) with scientific-visualization. (4) Compare expression between {conditions} if specified, applying suitable statistical tests with multiple-testing correction. (5) Identify tissue-specific versus ubiquitously expressed genes using a defined specificity metric (e.g. tau). (6) Report isoform-level expression differences where available. Validate a few genes against known tissue biology as a sanity check. Record database/release versions. Deliverables: save expression tables as CSV, heatmaps/plots as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report citing data sources and accessions; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations (cross-study batch effects, annotation coverage). If the organism or which conditions to compare is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: Protein Structure Analysis
description: Fetch and analyze PDB structures or AlphaFold predictions
prompt: Analyze the protein structure for: {protein}; first resolve it to a definitive identifier (UniProt accession and/or PDB ID), recording the organism and the source/version of the structure used. (1) Retrieve the structure from the PDB (note resolution, method, and any missing residues) or from AlphaFold if no experimental structure exists. (2) Describe the overall fold and domain architecture (cross-reference Pfam/InterPro). (3) Identify active-site residues and binding pockets, citing known catalytic/functional residues where available. (4) Analyze secondary-structure composition (DSSP via biopython). (5) Visualize key structural features (overall fold, pockets, key residues). (6) Compare with homologous structures via structural search/alignment if relevant. Validate annotations against UniProt feature records. Record database and tool versions. Deliverables: save figures as PNG, a residue/feature summary table as CSV, the retrieved coordinate file, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations (resolution, predicted-vs-experimental confidence). If the protein identity or which structure/conformation is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

placeholders.label: Protein name, UniProt ID, or PDB ID

name: Molecular Docking
description: Dock a ligand to a protein target and analyze binding
prompt: Perform molecular docking of {ligand} against {target}; first resolve {target} to a definitive structure (PDB ID or AlphaFold model) and {ligand} to a canonical SMILES/structure, recording organism and source versions. (1) Retrieve or prepare the protein structure (remove waters, add hydrogens, assign protonation at physiological pH, keep relevant cofactors). (2) Prepare the ligand (rdkit: sanitize, enumerate tautomers/stereoisomers, generate 3D conformers). (3) Define the binding site (known pocket residues, co-crystallized ligand, or blind docking) and report the chosen box/region. (4) Run docking (diffdock or a scoring-function docker) with a fixed seed and multiple poses/replicates. (5) Analyze binding poses and scores, checking pose plausibility and consistency. (6) Visualize the best binding mode with key interactions (H-bonds, hydrophobic, salt bridges). Validate by re-docking any known co-crystallized ligand as a positive control. Record tool/version. Deliverables: save pose structures, a ranked pose/score table as CSV, interaction figures as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report with predicted binding affinity and caveats; summarize in chat and name the files. If target or ligand identity is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: Mass Spectrometry Proteomics
description: Analyze LC-MS/MS proteomics data
prompt: Analyze the uploaded LC-MS/MS proteomics data; record the instrument/acquisition type, quantification scheme, and the {database} version used (set seeds where applicable). (1) Process raw spectra (peak picking, deconvolution) with pyopenms and report QC (TIC, identification rate, missed cleavages, mass accuracy). (2) Peptide-spectrum matching against the {database} database with a decoy database for target-decoy validation. (3) Protein inference with FDR control at the PSM/peptide/protein level (typically 1% FDR). (4) Label-free or TMT quantification with appropriate normalization and principled handling of missing values. (5) Differential expression between {comparison} using a suitable model (e.g. limma/moderated t-test) with BH FDR correction, flagging at FDR<0.05. (6) Pathway enrichment of significant proteins with FDR correction. Validate with expected housekeeping/spike-in controls and flag batch confounds. Generate a volcano plot and heatmap. Record tool/database versions. Deliverables: save protein/peptide and DE tables as CSV, figures as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations. If the database, quant scheme, or comparison is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: Protein-Protein Interaction Network
description: Build and analyze PPI networks
prompt: Construct a protein-protein interaction network for: {proteins}; first map inputs to stable identifiers (UniProt/STRING) for the correct organism, recording the STRING version and confidence cutoff used. (1) Query the STRING database for known interactions (bioservices), applying a defined confidence score threshold. (2) Add predicted interactions from co-expression and text-mining channels, distinguishing evidence types. (3) Build and visualize the network (networkx). (4) Identify hub proteins (degree/betweenness centrality) and detect clusters/modules (community detection). (5) Perform GO enrichment on each network module with FDR correction. (6) Compare with {context} if relevant (context-specific expression/disease genes). Validate that a few well-known interactions appear as a sanity check. Record database and tool versions. Deliverables: save the edge list, node-centrality, and module-enrichment tables as CSV, the network figure as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations (text-mining false positives, organism/context specificity). If the organism or which proteins are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

placeholders.label: Protein list (gene symbols or UniProt IDs)

name: AlphaFold Structure Prediction
description: Retrieve or predict protein 3D structure with AlphaFold
prompt: Obtain the AlphaFold-predicted structure for: {protein}; first resolve it to a UniProt accession, recording the organism and AlphaFold DB version. (1) Check the AlphaFold database for an existing prediction (bioservices); if absent, note that de novo prediction may be required and fetch the sequence. (2) Assess per-residue confidence (pLDDT) and, if available, the PAE matrix for inter-domain reliability. (3) Identify well-predicted (pLDDT>70/90) versus low-confidence/disordered regions. (4) Compare with experimental structures from the PDB if available (structural superposition, RMSD), noting agreement. (5) Analyze key structural features (domains, active sites, interfaces), cross-referencing UniProt/Pfam annotations. (6) Visualize and annotate the structure, coloring by pLDDT. Record database and tool versions. Deliverables: save the coordinate file, a per-region confidence table as CSV, annotated figures (including pLDDT coloring) as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations (single static model, low-confidence/disordered regions, no ligands/multimers unless modeled). If the protein identity is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: Protein Engineering Design
description: Design protein variants for improved function
prompt: Design protein engineering strategies for: {protein} to improve {property}; first resolve {protein} to a UniProt accession and reference structure, recording organism and source versions. (1) Analyze the wild-type structure and active/functional site (retrieve experimental or AlphaFold structure; map known functional residues). (2) Identify candidate mutagenesis positions using evolutionary conservation (MSA-derived), structural context (proximity to active site, surface vs core), and exclusion of catalytically essential residues for {property}. (3) Predict mutation effects using computational tools (ESM/inverse-folding likelihoods, stability predictors like FoldX/ddG), reporting uncertainty. (4) Rank mutations by predicted impact on {property}, separating beneficial from destabilizing. (5) Suggest a focused, combinatorial library design (positions, allowed substitutions, library size). (6) Recommend an experimental validation strategy (assay, controls including wild-type and known variants). Record tool/model and reference versions. Deliverables: save a ranked mutation/variant table as CSV, annotated structure figures as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report with rationale and caveats; summarize in chat and name the files. If the protein, the target property, or the activity assay is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: Binding Site Analysis
description: Characterize ligand binding pockets in a protein
prompt: Analyze the binding site(s) of: {protein}; first resolve it to a definitive structure (PDB ID or AlphaFold model), recording organism, resolution/confidence, and source version. (1) Identify all binding pockets using geometric and energy-based methods (e.g. fpocket/CASTp), reporting the detection method. (2) Characterize each pocket (volume, hydrophobicity, polarity, druggability score). (3) Identify key residues lining each binding site and cross-reference known functional residues from UniProt. (4) Map known ligands/co-crystallized molecules to the detected pockets as validation. (5) Compare pocket conservation across homologs (sequence/structure alignment) to flag specificity-determining residues. (6) Assess druggability and suggest pharmacophore features (H-bond donors/acceptors, hydrophobic anchors). Flag confounds (predicted-structure uncertainty, induced-fit/cryptic pockets). Record tool and database versions. Deliverables: save a pocket-characterization table and residue list as CSV, annotated pocket figures as PNG, the structure file, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations. If the protein identity or which structure to use is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: Molecular Dynamics Simulation
description: Set up and analyze MD simulations of biomolecules
prompt: Set up and analyze a molecular dynamics simulation of: {system}; first record the input structure source/version, force field, water model, and intended {duration}, and set random seeds for reproducibility. (1) Prepare the system: clean the structure, assign protonation states at physiological pH, parameterize any ligand, select an appropriate force field, then solvate and neutralize/ionize to physiological salt. (2) Energy minimization followed by staged NVT/NPT equilibration with position restraints, reporting temperature/pressure/density stability. (3) Production run setup for {duration} (default a sensible length if unset), noting integrator, timestep, and thermostat/barostat. (4) Analyze trajectory stability and dynamics: RMSD, per-residue RMSF, radius of gyration, and convergence checks. (5) Identify key conformational changes (clustering, PCA/essential dynamics). (6) Calculate binding free energy (MM/PBSA or similar) if a ligand is present, noting method assumptions. Validate equilibration before interpreting production. Record tool/force-field versions and seeds. Deliverables: save analysis plots as PNG, metric time-series tables as CSV, representative structures, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note sampling/timescale limitations. If the system or force field is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: Homology Modeling
description: Build a 3D model from a homologous template structure
prompt: Build a homology model for: {protein}; first resolve it to a sequence/UniProt accession, recording organism and template database version. (1) Search the PDB for suitable template structures (BLAST/HHsearch), selecting templates by sequence identity, coverage, and resolution, and reporting the chosen template(s). (2) Perform a careful target-template sequence alignment, manually checking conserved motifs and gap placement. (3) Build the 3D model using comparative modeling (e.g. MODELLER), generating multiple models. (4) Refine loop regions and side chains, especially low-identity and insertion regions. (5) Validate the model (Ramachandran plot, ERRAT, ProSA/QMEAN, DOPE score), reporting problematic regions. (6) Compare with the AlphaFold prediction if available (superposition, RMSD) as an independent check. Record tool and database versions. Deliverables: save the model coordinate file, a validation-metrics table as CSV, validation/structure figures as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report stating template identity and confidence; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations (low template identity, unreliable loops). If the protein identity is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: Protein Function Prediction
description: Predict function from sequence and structure
prompt: Predict the function of: {protein}; first record the organism (if known), sequence length, and reference database versions. (1) Run sequence similarity searches (BLAST against UniProt/NR, HMMer against profile databases), reporting top hits with identity, coverage, and E-values. (2) Identify conserved domains and families (InterPro, Pfam). (3) Predict subcellular localization (e.g. DeepLoc/signal-based tools). (4) Predict Gene Ontology terms (molecular function, biological process, cellular component) from sequence and, if a structure is available, structure-based signals. (5) Check for signal peptides (SignalP) and transmembrane domains (TMHMM/DeepTMHMM). (6) Integrate all evidence into a function prediction with explicit confidence scores, flagging conflicting evidence and low-similarity (twilight-zone) cases. Validate against any known annotation for close homologs. Record tool and database versions. Deliverables: save an evidence table (hits, domains, GO terms with scores) as CSV, summary figures (e.g. domain architecture) as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations of homology-based inference. If the protein identity or organism is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: PTM Analysis
description: Predict and analyze post-translational modifications
prompt: Analyze post-translational modifications for: {protein}; first resolve it to a UniProt accession, recording organism and database/tool versions. (1) Query known PTMs from UniProt and PhosphoSitePlus-style resources, recording evidence type. (2) Predict phosphorylation, glycosylation (N-/O-linked), ubiquitination, and acetylation sites with established predictors, reporting per-site confidence and distinguishing predicted from experimentally validated. (3) Map PTM sites onto the 3D structure (experimental or AlphaFold), checking surface accessibility. (4) Assess the structural and functional impact of each PTM (proximity to active sites, interfaces, disorder). (5) Check for disease-associated PTM changes (e.g. via variant/PTM databases). (6) Compare PTM-site conservation across species using an alignment as a sanity check. Record database and tool versions. Deliverables: save a PTM-site table (position, type, evidence, confidence) as CSV, annotated structure/sequence figures as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report distinguishing known vs predicted sites; summarize in chat, name the files, and note prediction false-positive caveats. If the protein identity or organism is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: Structural Alignment
description: Align and compare protein 3D structures
prompt: Perform structural alignment between: {structures}; first resolve each to a definitive PDB ID or model, recording organism, resolution/confidence, and chains/source versions used. (1) Superimpose the structures using TM-align (or DALI/CE for distant homologs), reporting the method and reference structure. (2) Calculate pairwise RMSD over aligned residues and TM-score (length-normalized) to quantify global similarity. (3) Identify conserved structural cores versus variable/flexible regions. (4) Map sequence differences onto the structural alignment (sequence vs structure agreement). (5) Highlight functional-site differences (active sites, binding pockets, interfaces). (6) Visualize the superposition with color-coded conserved/divergent regions and per-residue deviation. Sanity-check that closely related structures yield high TM-score/low RMSD. Record tool versions. Deliverables: save the superposed coordinates, an alignment-metrics table (RMSD, TM-score, aligned length) as CSV, annotated superposition figures as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report interpreting structural relationships; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations (chain/conformational-state choice, predicted-model confidence). If which structures/chains to compare is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

placeholders.label: PDB IDs or protein names to compare

name: Epitope Prediction
description: Predict B-cell and T-cell epitopes for vaccine design
prompt: Predict epitopes for: {antigen}; first resolve it to a sequence/UniProt accession, recording the organism, sequence source, and tool/database versions. (1) Predict linear B-cell epitopes from surface accessibility, hydrophilicity, and flexibility (e.g. BepiPred-style methods). (2) Predict conformational B-cell epitopes from the 3D structure (experimental or AlphaFold; e.g. DiscoTope), noting structure confidence. (3) Predict MHC-I (8-11mer) binding peptides for {hla_alleles} (default to a common-allele supertype panel if unspecified) using NetMHCpan-style tools. (4) Predict MHC-II binding peptides (15mer core). (5) Rank epitopes by predicted immunogenicity and binding affinity, and flag self-similarity/conservation across strains and potential allergenicity/toxicity as filters. (6) Map top epitopes onto the protein structure. Validate against any known epitopes (e.g. IEDB) as a positive control. Record tool and database versions. Deliverables: save ranked B-cell and T-cell epitope tables (sequence, position, allele, scores) as CSV, mapped-structure figures as PNG, and a Markdown methods+findings report suggesting prioritized vaccine candidates with caveats; summarize in chat and name the files. If the antigen identity or target HLA alleles are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions.

name: scRNA-seq Clustering
description: Identify and characterize cell clusters from scRNA-seq
prompt: Perform clustering on the uploaded scRNA-seq data. (1) QC: filter cells/genes on counts, n_genes, and mitochondrial % (default <15-20%, adjust per tissue), flag and remove doublets (Scrublet/scDblFinder). (2) Normalize (log1p or scran), select HVGs, scale, PCA; correct batch effects across samples (Harmony/scVI) and check the embedding for residual batch. (3) Run Leiden/Louvain at multiple resolutions; assess cluster stability (e.g., bootstrap/silhouette) and pick an optimal resolution. (4) Identify marker genes per cluster with the Wilcoxon rank-sum test, reporting effect sizes and BH-adjusted p-values; avoid double-dipping when interpreting. (5) Annotate clusters using {reference} markers and validate against known biology. (6) Visualize UMAP, marker expression, and report cluster proportions and distinguishing features. Set a random seed, record package versions. If an essential parameter (e.g., expected cell types or QC cutoffs) is ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions. Save figures (PNG), marker tables (CSV), the processed .h5ad, and a methods+findings report (Markdown); summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations.

placeholders.label: Reference markers or cell atlas to use

name: Cell Type Annotation
description: Automated cell type labeling using reference atlases
prompt: Annotate cell types in the uploaded scRNA-seq dataset. (1) Run QC and normalization first (mito %, doublet removal, log-normalization, HVGs), then automated annotation (scvi-tools/scANVI, CellTypist) against reference atlases for {tissue}, recording the reference and version. (2) Validate predictions with canonical marker genes for this tissue and known biology; flag batch effects between query and reference. (3) Resolve ambiguous or low-confidence calls with targeted subclustering and marker inspection. (4) Generate per-cell annotation confidence scores and report uncertain populations rather than over-labeling. (5) Visualize the annotated UMAP and dotplots of markers. (6) Report cell type proportions with counts. Set a random seed and record package versions. If the reference atlas or granularity (broad vs fine subtypes) is ambiguous and would materially change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions. Save figures (PNG), the annotation table (CSV), the labeled .h5ad, and a concise methods+findings report (Markdown); then summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations and low-confidence labels.

name: Trajectory / Pseudotime Analysis
description: Infer developmental trajectories and ordering of cells
prompt: Perform trajectory analysis on the uploaded scRNA-seq data. (1) Confirm upstream QC/normalization/batch correction, then identify the root cell population based on {root} using marker expression, not just position. (2) Compute diffusion pseudotime or PAGA connectivity (and RNA velocity with scVelo if spliced/unspliced counts exist) to order cells; verify the trajectory topology is biologically plausible and not driven by batch or cell-cycle. (3) Order cells along developmental trajectories. (4) Identify genes that change along pseudotime (trajectory-associated genes) with appropriate tests and multiple-comparison correction; report effect sizes. (5) Detect branch points and fate decisions, quantifying uncertainty where possible. (6) Visualize the trajectory on UMAP and gene expression trends along pseudotime. Set a random seed and record package versions. If the root identity or branching expectation is ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions. Save figures (PNG), trajectory gene tables (CSV), the processed .h5ad, and a methods+findings report (Markdown); summarize in chat, name files, and note limitations.

placeholders.label: Root cell type or marker (e.g., stem cells, progenitors)

name: Flow Cytometry Analysis
description: Gate, cluster, and quantify flow cytometry data
prompt: Analyze the uploaded flow cytometry data (FCS files). (1) Apply compensation/spillover correction and QC: remove debris/doublets (FSC-A vs FSC-H), gate on viability, check for time-based acquisition anomalies, and transform fluorescence (arcsinh/logicle). (2) Reproduce the manual gating strategy or run automated gating: {gating}. (3) Dimensionality reduction (UMAP) of marker expression. (4) Cluster with FlowSOM or PhenoGraph and annotate populations by canonical markers. (5) Quantify cell population frequencies per sample. (6) Compare populations between {comparison} using appropriate tests for the design (e.g., Mann-Whitney/t-test or mixed models for paired data) with multiple-comparison correction and effect sizes; flag batch/acquisition-day confounds. Set a random seed and record package versions. If the panel-to-marker mapping or comparison design is ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions. Generate publication-quality dot plots and histograms (PNG), frequency tables (CSV), and a methods+findings report (Markdown); summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations.

name: Cell-Cell Communication
description: Infer intercellular signaling from scRNA-seq
prompt: Infer cell-cell communication networks from the uploaded scRNA-seq data. (1) Confirm QC, normalization, and reliable cell-type annotations first, since communication inference is only as good as the labels. (2) Identify ligand-receptor pairs between cell types and score interaction strength using CellChat/CellPhoneDB/LIANA with permutation-based significance, reporting adjusted p-values. (3) Identify dominant signaling pathways. (4) Detect outgoing and incoming signals per cell type. (5) Visualize communication networks (chord diagrams, dot plots, heatmaps). (6) Compare signaling between {comparison} if multiple conditions, using differential interaction analysis and accounting for differences in cell-type abundance; flag that inferred interactions are predictions requiring spatial or functional validation. Set a random seed and record package/database versions. If conditions or the cell-type grouping are ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions. Save figures (PNG), interaction tables (CSV), the processed object, and a methods+findings report (Markdown); summarize in chat, name files, and note limitations.

name: Multi-Omics Integration
description: Integrate transcriptomics, proteomics, and other omics layers
prompt: Integrate the uploaded multi-omics datasets ({modalities}). (1) Run per-modality QC and normalization appropriate to each layer (e.g., log-norm for RNA, TF-IDF/LSI for ATAC, CLR for protein), then harmonize features and cells across modalities. (2) Apply integration methods (MOFA+, Seurat WNN, or multimodal scVI/totalVI/MultiVI), correcting batch effects and recording the method and parameters. (3) Generate a joint embedding and check that it reflects shared biology rather than technical or batch structure. (4) Identify features driving cross-modality variation (factor loadings, modality weights). (5) Discover multi-omic cell states or subtypes via joint clustering. (6) Validate with modality-specific markers and known biology. Set a random seed and record package versions. If the integration target or batch keys are ambiguous and would materially change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions. Save figures (PNG), feature/factor tables (CSV), the integrated object (e.g., .h5ad/.h5mu), and a concise methods+findings report (Markdown); summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations.

placeholders.label: Data modalities (e.g., RNA + ATAC, RNA + protein)

name: Cell Cycle Analysis
description: Score cell cycle phases and regress out cell cycle effects
prompt: Perform cell cycle analysis on the uploaded scRNA-seq data. (1) After normalization, score G1, S, and G2M phases using standard gene sets (e.g., Tirosh S/G2M genes), mapping to the correct species orthologs. (2) Visualize the cell cycle distribution across clusters and on UMAP. (3) Test whether cell cycle drives clustering (e.g., compare embeddings/cluster assignments before vs after, association tests). (4) Regress out or correct cell cycle effects only if it is a confounder, and report the impact; avoid removing biologically meaningful proliferation signal. (5) Compare cell cycle phase distributions between {comparison} using appropriate proportion tests with multiple-comparison correction and effect sizes. (6) Identify cell cycle-regulated genes specific to each cell type. Set a random seed and record package versions. If the comparison groups or species are ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions. Save figures (PNG), phase/score tables (CSV), the processed .h5ad, and a methods+findings report (Markdown); summarize in chat, name files, and note limitations.

name: Perturbation / CRISPR Screen Analysis
description: Analyze Perturb-seq or CRISPR screen data
prompt: Analyze the uploaded perturbation screen data ({screen_type}). (1) QC: assess guide RNA assignment, coverage, and cell numbers per perturbation, filter low-coverage guides, run standard scRNA QC (mito %, doublets), and handle multiplets/multiple-guide cells. (2) Identify differentially expressed genes per perturbation vs non-targeting controls using a suitable model (e.g., negative-binomial/pseudobulk DE) with BH correction and reported effect sizes; avoid treating each cell as independent when pseudobulk is appropriate. (3) Score perturbation effects on gene programs/signatures. (4) Cluster perturbations by transcriptional similarity. (5) Identify genetic interactions or epistasis where the design supports it. (6) Prioritize hits for validation with explicit criteria and confidence. Set a random seed and record package versions. If the control definition or replicate structure is ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions. Save figures (PNG), per-perturbation DE and hit tables (CSV), the processed object, and a methods+findings report (Markdown); summarize in chat, name files, and note limitations.

placeholders.label: Screen type (Perturb-seq, CRISPRi, CRISPRa, CRISPR KO)

name: Immune Cell Profiling
description: Deep characterization of immune cell populations
prompt: Perform immune cell profiling from the uploaded data ({data_type}). (1) Apply assay-appropriate QC/preprocessing (scRNA: mito %, doublets, normalization, HVGs, batch correction; CyTOF/flow: compensation, transformation, debris/viability gating), then identify major immune populations (T cells, B cells, NK, myeloid, DCs). (2) Subtype each population (CD4/CD8, naive/memory/effector) and validate with canonical markers. (3) Score activation, exhaustion, and cytotoxicity signatures using established gene/marker sets. (4) Quantify clonal expansion from TCR/BCR if available. (5) Compare immune composition between {comparison} using appropriate tests for the design with multiple-comparison correction and effect sizes; flag batch and patient-level confounds. (6) Generate a comprehensive immune landscape report. Set a random seed and record package versions. If the comparison design or subtype granularity is ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions. Save figures (PNG), composition/signature tables (CSV), the processed object, and a methods+findings report (Markdown); summarize in chat, name files, and note limitations.

placeholders.label: Groups to compare (e.g., responders vs non-responders)

name: Lineage Tracing Analysis
description: Reconstruct cell lineage trees from barcoding data
prompt: Analyze lineage tracing data from the uploaded dataset. (1) Process barcode or mutation data for lineage reconstruction, with QC on barcode detection/dropout and collision filtering. (2) Build lineage trees using neighbor-joining or Bayesian/maximum-likelihood methods, reporting branch support where available. (3) Map cell types onto the lineage tree, integrating paired transcriptomic annotations if present. (4) Identify lineage-biased progenitors. (5) Quantify fate probabilities for each progenitor class with uncertainty estimates. (6) Visualize lineage trees annotated with cell types and pseudotime, and check that inferred topology is robust to barcode noise. Set a random seed and record package versions. If the barcode encoding scheme or expected lineage structure is ambiguous and would materially change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions. Save figures (PNG), tree files and fate-probability tables (CSV), the processed object, and a concise methods+findings report (Markdown); then summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations including barcode homoplasy and dropout.

name: Spatial Cell Analysis
description: Analyze cell spatial organization and neighborhoods
prompt: Analyze spatial cell organization in the uploaded data ({platform}). (1) Segment cells, QC segmentation quality, and quantify marker expression with platform-appropriate normalization (and background/autofluorescence handling for IMC/mIF; transcript QC for MERFISH/Xenium/CosMx). (2) Classify cell types and validate with canonical markers. (3) Identify spatial neighborhoods and niches (e.g., neighborhood enrichment, niche clustering). (4) Compute spatial statistics (co-localization, exclusion, clustering) with permutation-based significance and multiple-comparison correction. (5) Detect tissue microenvironments. (6) Correlate spatial patterns with {outcome} if available, using appropriate tests and reporting effect sizes; flag batch/slide and segmentation confounds. Set a random seed and record package versions. If the cell-type scheme or outcome variable is ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions. Save figures including spatial overlays (PNG), neighborhood/statistics tables (CSV), the processed object, and a methods+findings report (Markdown); summarize in chat, name files, and note limitations.

name: Molecular Property Prediction
description: Predict physicochemical and biological properties of molecules
prompt: Predict properties of the uploaded molecules (SMILES or SDF). Properties to predict: {properties}. First parse and standardize all structures (RDKit sanitization, salt stripping, charge/tautomer normalization) and drop or flag invalid entries. (1) Compute descriptors (MW, LogP, TPSA, HBD/HBA, rotatable bonds, aromatic rings). (2) Apply validated models for the requested properties, naming each method/tool and version; treat outputs as predictions with uncertainty, not measurements. (3) Assess drug-likeness (Lipinski, Veber, Ghose). (4) Note the applicability domain and flag out-of-domain or unusual structures. (5) Set a random seed for reproducibility. If a requested property is ambiguous or lacks a sound model, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a results CSV (IDs + SMILES + predictions), per-molecule property dashboards as PNG, and a concise Markdown report. Summarize key findings and outliers in chat, name the files, and state limitations.

placeholders.label: Properties to predict (e.g., solubility, toxicity, pKa)

name: Chemical Reaction Prediction
description: Predict products, yields, or conditions for chemical reactions
prompt: Predict the products and conditions for the reaction: {reaction}. First parse and standardize reactant SMILES (sanitize, balance atoms, note any ambiguity). (1) Identify reaction type and plausible mechanism. (2) Predict major and minor products with structures. (3) Estimate regio- and stereoselectivity, stating the basis. (4) Suggest reaction conditions (solvent, temperature, catalyst, base) with literature precedent where available. (5) Give an expected yield range, framed as an estimate with uncertainty, not a guarantee. (6) Flag side reactions and safety/hazard concerns. Name the tools/models and versions used; record a seed if any stochastic step is involved. If the substrate, reagents, or intent are ambiguous enough to change products, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save predicted reaction schemes/structures as PNG, a products table as CSV (SMILES + role + confidence), and a Markdown report. Summarize top products and caveats in chat and name the files.

placeholders.label: Reaction SMILES, description, or uploaded reaction scheme

name: Retrosynthesis Planning
description: Plan synthetic routes to a target molecule
prompt: Plan a retrosynthetic route to: {target}. First resolve and standardize the target structure (canonical SMILES, sanitization) and confirm stereochemistry. (1) Identify strategic disconnections. (2) Propose 3-5 distinct synthetic routes. (3) Evaluate each on step count, building-block/reagent availability, expected yields, and convergence. (4) Check literature/databases for precedent reactions and cite sources. (5) Rank routes by overall practicality and risk. (6) Give a detailed forward synthesis for the top route with specific reagents, conditions, and key intermediates. Treat predicted feasibility and yields as hypotheses requiring experimental confirmation; name tools/databases and versions used. If the target identity or constraints (scale, chirality, available starting materials) are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save route schemes as PNG, a route-comparison table as CSV (steps, yield, availability, score), and a Markdown report. Summarize the recommended route and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Conformer Generation & Analysis
description: Generate and analyze 3D molecular conformations
prompt: Generate and analyze conformers for: {molecule}. First resolve the structure to canonical SMILES, sanitize, add explicit Hs, and assign stereochemistry. (1) Generate a diverse conformer ensemble (ETKDG) with a fixed random seed for reproducibility. (2) Optimize geometries with a force field (e.g., MMFF94/UFF), noting the method and version. (3) Rank conformers by energy and deduplicate by RMSD. (4) Analyze the conformational landscape (key torsions, RMSD clustering, energy spread). (5) Identify the global minimum and key metastable states, noting that force-field energies are approximate and may need QM refinement. (6) Visualize low-energy conformers. If the input is ambiguous (protonation/tautomer state, stereochemistry) and would change the ensemble, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save conformers as SDF, ensemble/torsion plots and conformer renders as PNG, an energy-ranking table as CSV, and a Markdown report. Summarize the minimum and accessible states in chat and name the files.

name: QSAR Modeling
description: Build quantitative structure-activity relationship models
prompt: Build a QSAR model for {target_property} using the uploaded molecular dataset. First standardize all structures (sanitize, strip salts, normalize charges/tautomers), deduplicate, and validate/transform the activity column (e.g., pIC50). (1) Compute descriptors and fingerprints. (2) Split using scaffold (Bemis-Murcko) splits to avoid train/test leakage, with a fixed seed. (3) Train multiple models (random forest, gradient boosting, neural network). (4) Evaluate with nested or scaffold cross-validation reporting R2, RMSE, MAE with uncertainty/confidence intervals. (5) Analyze feature importance and define the applicability domain. (6) Predict {target_property} for new molecules if provided, flagging out-of-domain predictions as low-confidence. Record package versions and seeds. If the endpoint, units, or activity column are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save predictions/metrics as CSV (IDs + SMILES), performance and importance plots as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize best model, metrics, and limitations in chat and name the files.

placeholders.label: Target property (e.g., IC50, EC50, solubility, permeability)

name: Chemical Space Exploration
description: Visualize and navigate chemical space of a compound library
prompt: Explore the chemical space of the uploaded compound library. First standardize structures (sanitize, strip salts, normalize) and deduplicate. (1) Compute molecular fingerprints (e.g., ECFP) for all compounds. (2) Reduce to 2D/3D with t-SNE and UMAP, fixing random seeds and reporting key parameters (perplexity, n_neighbors). (3) Color by {property} if available. (4) Identify clusters and outliers. (5) Compare with known drug space (e.g., DrugBank) to contextualize coverage. (6) Identify underexplored regions. (7) Report diversity metrics (scaffold diversity, internal similarity, property coverage). Note that embeddings are non-deterministic across parameter choices and are exploratory, not definitive; record package versions. If the library format or the intended {property} coloring is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save embedding and cluster figures as PNG, a per-compound table as CSV (IDs + SMILES + coordinates + cluster), and a Markdown report. Summarize structure, clusters, and gaps in chat and name the files.

placeholders.label: Property to color by (optional, e.g., activity, source)

name: NMR Spectral Analysis
description: Interpret and assign NMR spectra
prompt: Analyze the uploaded NMR spectrum ({nmr_type}) for: {compound}. First confirm the data format, solvent, and reference; if a structure is provided, standardize it (canonical SMILES). (1) Identify all peaks and chemical shifts. (2) Assign peaks to structural features, integrating where applicable. (3) Analyze multiplicity/coupling (1H) or DEPT editing (13C). (4) Determine connectivity from 2D experiments if provided (COSY, HSQC, HMBC). (5) Confirm or propose the molecular structure, noting alternative assignments and ambiguities; predicted shifts (name tool/version) are estimates with error. (6) Report a complete assignments table. Treat any structure elucidation as a hypothesis needing orthogonal confirmation. If {compound} is unknown or the experiment type/conditions are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save an annotated spectrum as PNG, the assignments table as CSV, and a Markdown report. Summarize the assignment and confidence in chat and name the files.

name: Mass Spectrometry Analysis
description: Analyze and interpret mass spectra
prompt: Analyze the uploaded mass spectrometry data ({ms_type}). First confirm polarity, mass accuracy/resolution, and calibration. (1) Identify the molecular ion/adduct and derive candidate molecular formulae within a stated ppm tolerance. (2) Interpret the fragmentation pattern and rationalize key losses. (3) Match fragments and spectra against libraries (e.g., MassBank, mzCloud) with match scores. (4) Propose structural fragments for unassigned peaks, marked as tentative. (5) Quantify target compounds if standards/calibration provided, reporting uncertainty. (6) Generate an annotated spectrum. Treat formula and structure assignments as hypotheses with confidence levels, not certainties; name tools/databases and versions. If adduct, polarity, or instrument details are ambiguous and would change formulae, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save the annotated spectrum as PNG, a peak/annotation table as CSV (m/z, intensity, formula, score), and a Markdown report. Summarize identifications and caveats in chat and name the files.

placeholders.label: MS type (ESI, MALDI, EI, LC-MS/MS, GC-MS)

name: Thermodynamics Calculation
description: Calculate thermodynamic properties and equilibria
prompt: Calculate thermodynamic properties for: {system}. First specify states, phases, stoichiometry, and the data source for thermochemical values (e.g., NIST), citing them. (1) Compute standard enthalpies, entropies, and Gibbs free energies of reaction. (2) Determine equilibrium constants at {temperature} (default 298.15 K). (3) Generate van't Hoff plots across a sensible temperature range, noting the assumption of temperature-independent dH/dS and its limits. (4) Assess spontaneity and feasibility. (5) Estimate activation energy if kinetic data are provided. (6) Compare with experimental literature values and report discrepancies. Propagate uncertainty from input data and state assumptions; record any package versions and seeds. If the system, phases, or conditions are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save van't Hoff and related plots as PNG, a results table as CSV (quantity, value, uncertainty, source), and a Markdown report. Summarize key thermodynamic conclusions and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: pKa Prediction
description: Predict acid dissociation constants for molecules
prompt: Predict pKa values for: {molecule}. First resolve and standardize the structure (canonical SMILES, sanitize, neutralize, enumerate relevant tautomers). (1) Identify all ionizable groups. (2) Predict microscopic pKa for each site, naming the method/tool and version and treating values as predictions with error bars. (3) Determine the dominant protonation/charge state at pH {ph} (default 7.4). (4) Generate a microspeciation diagram across pH. (5) Compare with experimental pKa if available and report deviations. (6) Analyze how pH affects charge state and solubility. Note model applicability limits for unusual scaffolds. If the structure, tautomer, or pH of interest is ambiguous and would change conclusions, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save the microspeciation diagram as PNG, a pKa/site table as CSV (site, pKa, type), and a Markdown report. Summarize the dominant species at the target pH and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Solubility Prediction
description: Predict aqueous solubility and formulation considerations
prompt: Predict the aqueous solubility of: {molecules}. First parse and standardize all structures (sanitize, strip salts, normalize charges/tautomers) and flag invalid entries. (1) Calculate LogS using multiple methods (e.g., ESOL, group contribution, ML), naming each tool/version and treating outputs as predictions with uncertainty, not measurements. (2) Predict intrinsic and pH-dependent solubility, noting required pKa assumptions. (3) Analyze structural features driving solubility. (4) Suggest salt forms or co-crystal strategies to improve solubility where needed. (5) Compare with experimental data if available and report deviations. (6) Report BCS classification (with the permeability assumption stated). Note applicability-domain limits and set a seed if models are stochastic. If the input set or pH context is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a results CSV (IDs + SMILES + LogS + method), comparison/feature plots as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize key solubility findings and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Reaction Optimization
description: Design experiments to optimize reaction conditions
prompt: Design an optimization campaign for the reaction: {reaction}. First confirm the response/objective metric (yield, selectivity, conversion) and standardize any reactant structures. (1) Identify key variables and ranges (temperature, solvent, catalyst loading, concentration, time). (2) Design a DoE plan (factorial/response-surface) or a Bayesian optimization loop, fixing a random seed and stating priors/factor encodings. (3) Suggest an initial screening set of experiments. (4) Define the objective function and constraints (cost, safety, practicality). (5) Propose an iterative strategy with stopping criteria. (6) Estimate the number of experiments needed and expected information gain. Treat predicted optima as hypotheses to verify experimentally; name tools and versions. If the objective or controllable variables are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save the proposed design matrix as CSV, design-space/response plots as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize the recommended plan and assumptions in chat and name the files.

name: Polymer Design & Analysis
description: Design polymers with target properties
prompt: Design or analyze a polymer for {application}. Target properties: {properties}. First clarify constraints (cost, processing, regulatory, biodegradability) and standardize any monomer structures (canonical SMILES). (1) Identify candidate monomer/repeat-unit structures. (2) Predict key polymer properties (Tg, Tm, mechanical strength, solubility), naming methods/tools and versions and treating values as estimates with uncertainty, not measurements. (3) Evaluate sustainability and processability. (4) Compare with existing polymers from the literature, citing sources. (5) Suggest plausible synthesis/polymerization routes. (6) Recommend characterization experiments to validate predictions. Note that empirical property models for polymers carry substantial error; set a seed for any stochastic step. If the application or target properties are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save candidate structures/property plots as PNG, a candidate-comparison table as CSV (monomer SMILES + predicted properties), and a Markdown report. Summarize top candidates and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Catalyst Design
description: Design and screen catalysts for chemical reactions
prompt: Design a catalyst for: {reaction}. First standardize reactant/product structures and define the performance metric (activity, selectivity, turnover). (1) Analyze the mechanism and identify the rate-determining step. (2) Propose candidate catalyst classes (metal-organic, enzymatic, organocatalytic) with rationale. (3) Predict activity/selectivity trends using descriptors or scaling relationships, naming the approach. (4) Screen candidates computationally, recording method, level of theory/model, and versions. (5) Compare with known catalysts from the literature, citing sources. (6) Recommend top candidates for experimental testing. Treat computed activity as a hypothesis with error, not a guarantee, and note scoring/descriptor limitations; set seeds where applicable. If the target reaction, conditions, or selectivity goal are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save candidate structures and trend plots as PNG, a ranking table as CSV (catalyst, descriptor, predicted performance), and a Markdown report. Summarize top candidates and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Chemical Safety Assessment
description: Evaluate hazards and safety of chemical compounds
prompt: Perform a safety assessment of: {chemicals}. First resolve each input (name/CAS/SMILES) to a standardized structure and confirm identity. (1) Look up GHS classification and hazard statements from authoritative sources, citing them. (2) Predict toxicity endpoints (LD50, LC50, mutagenicity), naming tools/versions and treating predictions as estimates with uncertainty, not regulatory determinations. (3) Assess environmental fate (biodegradation, bioaccumulation). (4) Check regulatory status (REACH, TSCA). (5) Identify incompatibilities and storage/handling requirements. (6) Compile a safety data summary. Clearly distinguish authoritative/experimental data from in silico predictions; note this is informational and not a substitute for an official SDS or expert review. If a chemical identity is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a structured summary table as CSV (chemical, GHS, predicted endpoints, sources), hazard visuals as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize key hazards and caveats in chat and name the files.

placeholders.label: Chemical names, CAS numbers, or SMILES

name: Metabolite Identification
description: Identify metabolites from mass spectrometry data
prompt: Identify metabolites from the uploaded {data_type} data. First confirm acquisition mode, mass accuracy, and adduct/ionization assumptions. (1) Process and align spectral features (peak picking, deisotoping). (2) Match against spectral databases (HMDB, MassBank, METLIN) with similarity scores. (3) Predict identities from accurate mass and fragmentation within a stated tolerance. (4) Assign confidence levels per the Metabolomics Standards Initiative (identified, putative, unknown). (5) Map identified metabolites to pathways (e.g., KEGG). (6) Report a ranked candidate list per feature. Treat putative IDs as hypotheses requiring authentic-standard confirmation; name tools/databases and versions. If the data type, polarity, or adduct context is ambiguous and would change matches, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a ranked candidate table as CSV (feature, m/z, candidate, score, confidence), annotated spectra/pathway figures as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize confident identifications and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Synthetic Accessibility Score
description: Evaluate how easy compounds are to synthesize
prompt: Evaluate the synthetic accessibility of the uploaded molecules. First parse and standardize all structures (sanitize, strip salts, normalize) and flag invalid entries. (1) Calculate SA scores (e.g., SAScore) for each compound, naming the method/version. (2) Identify challenging motifs (strained rings, dense stereocenters, rare functional groups). (3) Estimate the number of synthetic steps, framed as a rough indicator with uncertainty. (4) Flag commercially available building blocks where identifiable, citing the source. (5) Rank compounds by ease of synthesis. (6) Suggest simplifications for hard-to-make compounds while preserving key features. Note that SA scores are heuristic proxies, not route guarantees, and depend on the scoring model; set a seed if any step is stochastic. If structures are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a ranking table as CSV (ID + SMILES + SA score + flags), example structures as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize easiest/hardest compounds and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Drug Target Identification
description: Identify and validate therapeutic targets for a disease
prompt: Identify potential drug targets for: {disease}. First confirm the precise indication/subtype and scope. (1) Query disease-gene associations from OpenTargets, OMIM, and the GWAS catalog, citing evidence. (2) Prioritize targets by genetic evidence strength, expression, and druggability. (3) Check existing drugs/programs per target. (4) Assess tractability (structure availability, assayability, ligandability). (5) Evaluate safety liabilities (tissue expression breadth, mouse KO phenotypes). (6) Recommend the top 5-10 targets with explicit rationale and evidence grade. Distinguish genetic/experimental evidence from computational inference; treat prioritization as hypothesis-generating, not validated. Name databases and versions/access dates. If the indication is ambiguous or overly broad, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a ranked target table as CSV (target, evidence, druggability, safety flags), evidence/expression plots as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize the top targets and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Virtual Screening
description: Screen compound libraries against a target
prompt: Perform virtual screening against {target}. First validate the prepared target: obtain/clean structure (PDB: {pdb_id}), fix protonation, add hydrogens, remove waters/artifacts, and validate the binding site (ideally redock a known ligand to check pose recovery). (1) Prepare the target structure. (2) Define the binding site. (3) Standardize and dock the uploaded compound library or a ZINC subset ({library_size} compounds), generating reasonable 3D states/protonation; record docking tool and version and a seed. (4) Rank by docking score, noting that scoring functions are noisy and poorly correlated with true affinity. (5) Apply ADMET filters and PAINS/structural alerts. (6) Cluster top hits by scaffold for diversity. (7) Select a diverse top 50 for experimental testing. Treat all hits as hypotheses requiring assay confirmation. If the target/site or library is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a ranked hits CSV (ID + SMILES + score + filters), pose/cluster figures as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize top candidates and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: ADMET Prediction
description: Predict absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion, and toxicity
prompt: Predict ADMET properties for the uploaded compounds. First parse and standardize all structures (sanitize, strip salts, normalize charges/tautomers) and flag invalid entries.
