(1) Absorption: Caco-2 permeability, oral bioavailability, P-gp substrate. (2) Distribution: plasma protein binding, VDss, BBB penetration. (3) Metabolism: CYP inhibition/induction (1A2, 2C9, 2C19, 2D6, 3A4), metabolic stability. (4) Excretion: clearance, half-life. (5) Toxicity: hERG, AMES, hepatotoxicity. (6) Generate a traffic-light dashboard with per-endpoint confidence/applicability-domain flags. Name each model/tool and version, set a seed, and treat every value as an in silico prediction with error, not a measurement; flag out-of-domain compounds as low-confidence. If structures or the compound set are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a results CSV (ID + SMILES + endpoints + confidence), the dashboard as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize key liabilities and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Lead Optimization
description: Optimize a lead compound for potency, selectivity, and ADMET
prompt: Optimize the lead compound: {compound} for {target}. First standardize the lead and any analog data (canonical SMILES, sanitize) and check the SAR dataset for quality and leakage. (1) Analyze the SAR from the uploaded data. (2) Identify key pharmacophore features and liabilities. (3) Propose structural modifications to improve {property} while monitoring potency, selectivity, and ADMET trade-offs. (4) Predict ADMET and, where possible, activity for proposed analogs, naming tools/versions and treating outputs as predictions with uncertainty. (5) Rank modifications by predicted improvement and synthetic accessibility, flagging out-of-domain designs. (6) Design a focused library of 20-50 analogs for synthesis. Set seeds for reproducibility; treat all designs as testable hypotheses. If {target}, the optimization goal, or the SAR data are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save the analog library as CSV (SMILES + predicted properties + rationale), SAR/property plots as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize top analogs and caveats in chat and name the files.

placeholders.label: Property to optimize (potency, selectivity, solubility, metabolic stability)

name: Drug Repurposing
description: Identify existing drugs for new therapeutic indications
prompt: Identify drug repurposing candidates for: {disease}. First confirm the precise indication and mechanistic hypothesis of interest. (1) Query drug-disease associations from knowledge graphs (PrimeKG) and databases, citing evidence. (2) Identify approved drugs targeting related pathways/targets. (3) Check clinical trials and the literature for off-label or prior use. (4) Predict drug-target interactions computationally, naming tools/versions and treating predictions as hypotheses with uncertainty. (5) Assess safety profiles from existing clinical data. (6) Rank candidates by evidence strength, mechanistic plausibility, and feasibility. Distinguish established clinical evidence from computational inference; note that repurposing requires prospective validation. Record database versions/access dates. If the indication is ambiguous or broad, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a ranked candidate table as CSV (drug, target, evidence, safety, score), an evidence network/plot as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize the top candidates and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: PK Modeling
description: Pharmacokinetic modeling and simulation
prompt: Build a pharmacokinetic model for {drug} using the uploaded PK data. First inspect the data (units, dosing, sampling design, BLQ handling) and confirm the dosing route. (1) Fit compartmental models (1- and 2-compartment), comparing by AIC/BIC and goodness-of-fit. (2) Estimate PK parameters (Cmax, Tmax, AUC, half-life, clearance, Vd) with confidence intervals. (3) Test for dose linearity. (4) Simulate concentration-time profiles at doses: {doses}. (5) Predict steady-state concentrations for repeated dosing, stating accumulation assumptions. (6) Generate PK profile plots with observed-vs-predicted overlays. Report parameter uncertainty and model diagnostics (residuals); record package versions and a seed for any simulation. Note that extrapolations beyond observed data are uncertain. If dosing, units, or design are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a parameter table as CSV (with CIs), PK and diagnostic plots as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize key parameters and caveats in chat and name the files.

placeholders.label: Doses to simulate (e.g., 10 mg, 50 mg, 100 mg)

name: Dose-Response Analysis
description: Fit dose-response curves and calculate EC50/IC50
prompt: Analyze the uploaded dose-response data. First inspect the data (replicates, controls, normalization, units) and confirm orientation (agonist vs inhibitor). (1) Fit a 4-parameter logistic (Hill) curve per compound, handling normalization and constraints appropriately. (2) Calculate EC50/IC50 with 95% confidence intervals. (3) Determine the Hill coefficient and maximal response. (4) Compare potency across compounds, noting overlapping CIs. (5) Assess curve quality (R2, residual plots, convergence) and flag poorly constrained fits (e.g., incomplete curves). (6) Generate publication-quality dose-response plots with error bars on a log-dose axis. Report uncertainty rather than point estimates alone; record package versions and a seed for any resampling. If data layout or assay direction is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a results table as CSV (compound, EC50/IC50, CI, Hill, R2), fitted-curve plots as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize potency ranking and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Drug Interaction Prediction
description: Predict drug-drug interactions and mechanisms
prompt: Predict drug-drug interactions for: {drugs}. First resolve each drug to a standardized identity/structure and confirm the clinical context if relevant. (1) Check known interactions in DrugBank and authoritative sources, citing them. (2) Predict CYP-mediated metabolic interactions (inhibition, induction), naming the basis. (3) Check transporter-mediated interactions (P-gp, OATP, BCRP). (4) Assess pharmacodynamic interactions (additive, synergistic, antagonistic). (5) Evaluate clinical significance and likely management. (6) Compile an interaction matrix. Distinguish documented interactions from in silico predictions, which carry uncertainty; note this is decision-support, not a substitute for clinical judgment or a clinical pharmacist. Record tool/database versions. If a drug identity is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save the interaction matrix as CSV (pair, mechanism, severity, evidence), a heatmap as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize the most clinically significant interactions and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Clinical Trial Design
description: Design a clinical trial protocol
prompt: Design a clinical trial for {drug} in {indication}, following ICH-GCP guidelines. First confirm the phase, population, and primary objective. (1) Define primary and secondary endpoints with measurement methods. (2) Calculate sample size via power analysis, stating effect size, alpha/power, dropout, and the test used. (3) Describe study design (parallel, crossover, adaptive) and randomization/blinding. (4) Define inclusion/exclusion criteria. (5) Plan the dosing regimen and schedule. (6) Describe the safety monitoring plan (DSMB, stopping rules, AE reporting). (7) Outline the statistical analysis plan (analysis sets, handling of missing data, multiplicity). State all assumptions behind the sample-size calculation; cite guidance/precedent trials. Treat this as a draft protocol skeleton requiring biostatistician and regulatory review. If phase or population is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a design-summary table and sample-size scenarios as CSV, a schema/power-curve figure as PNG, and a Markdown protocol synopsis. Summarize key design choices and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Biomarker Discovery
description: Identify and validate biomarkers from omics data
prompt: Discover biomarkers for {condition} from the uploaded {data_type} data. First inspect the data (batch structure, missingness, normalization, class balance) and define groups. (1) Differential analysis between groups with appropriate multiple-testing control. (2) Feature selection (LASSO, random-forest importance, mutual information) performed inside cross-validation folds to avoid selection leakage, with a fixed seed. (3) Build a multi-marker panel classifier. (4) Evaluate with ROC/AUC under nested cross-validation, reporting confidence intervals. (5) Validate on independent data if available; otherwise flag overfitting risk. (6) Assess biological plausibility of top markers via pathway analysis (with SHAP for interpretation). Report uncertainty and distinguish discovery from validation; record package versions. If groups or the endpoint are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a ranked marker table as CSV (feature, effect, importance, p/FDR), ROC and importance plots as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize the candidate panel, performance, and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Toxicity Prediction
description: Predict compound toxicity and safety liabilities
prompt: Predict toxicity for the uploaded compounds. First parse and standardize all structures (sanitize, strip salts, normalize charges/tautomers) and flag invalid entries. (1) AMES mutagenicity. (2) hERG channel inhibition (cardiac toxicity). (3) Hepatotoxicity (DILI risk). (4) Skin sensitization. (5) Acute oral toxicity (LD50). (6) Carcinogenicity. (7) Endocrine-disruption potential. Generate a traffic-light safety dashboard with per-endpoint confidence and applicability-domain flags. Name each model/tool and version, set a seed, and report structural alerts where relevant. Treat all outputs as in silico predictions with error, not regulatory or experimental conclusions; flag out-of-domain compounds as low-confidence and recommend experimental confirmation for high-risk flags. If structures or the compound set are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a results CSV (ID + SMILES + endpoints + confidence + alerts), the dashboard as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize the highest-risk compounds and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Pharmacogenomics Analysis
description: Analyze genetic variants affecting drug response
prompt: Perform pharmacogenomics analysis for {drug}. First confirm the clinical context and the gene(s)/variants of interest. (1) Identify pharmacogenomic variants from ClinPGx and PharmGKB, citing evidence levels. (2) Assess how {genotype} variants affect drug metabolism, efficacy, and toxicity. (3) Review FDA pharmacogenomic labeling and CPIC guidance. (4) Predict metabolizer status (poor, intermediate, extensive, ultra-rapid), stating the diplotype-to-phenotype basis. (5) Recommend dosing adjustments per published guidelines. (6) Compile a clinical PGx report. Distinguish guideline-backed recommendations from inference; note this is decision-support requiring clinician interpretation, and record database versions/access dates. If the drug or genotype is ambiguous and would change recommendations, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a variant/recommendation table as CSV (gene, variant, phenotype, guidance, evidence), a summary figure as PNG, and a Markdown PGx report. Summarize phenotype, dosing guidance, and caveats in chat and name the files.

placeholders.label: Patient genotype or gene of interest (e.g., CYP2D6)

name: Drug-Target Interaction Network
description: Map the polypharmacology network of compounds
prompt: Build a drug-target interaction network for: {compounds}. First resolve and standardize each compound (canonical SMILES) and confirm identities. (1) Query known targets from ChEMBL, DrugBank, and BindingDB, recording assay/activity values and citing sources. (2) Predict additional targets computationally, naming tools/versions and treating predictions as hypotheses with uncertainty. (3) Construct the drug-target network. (4) Identify off-target effects and polypharmacology. (5) Enrich targets with pathway information. (6) Visualize the network and distinguish therapeutic vs safety-relevant targets. Separate experimentally documented interactions from in silico predictions; note that predicted off-targets need experimental confirmation. Record database versions/access dates and a seed for any stochastic prediction. If a compound identity is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save an edge list as CSV (compound, target, evidence, activity, source), the network figure as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize key targets, polypharmacology, and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Pharma Competitive Landscape
description: Analyze the competitive landscape for a therapeutic area
prompt: Analyze the competitive landscape for {indication}. First confirm the precise scope (geography, modalities, line of therapy). (1) Map drugs in development (preclinical through Phase 3) from ClinicalTrials.gov and other sources, citing them. (2) Categorize by mechanism of action. (3) Identify the most advanced programs and likely timelines. (4) Analyze patent-expiry timelines for key incumbents. (5) Assess market size and pricing, stating sources and assumptions. (6) Identify white-space opportunities and unmet needs. Distinguish primary-source facts from estimates/inference and note data recency (access dates); market and timeline figures are uncertain projections, not guarantees. If the indication or scope is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a pipeline table as CSV (program, sponsor, MoA, phase, status, source), landscape/timeline figures as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize the most advanced programs, white space, and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Side Effect Prediction
description: Predict adverse drug reactions from molecular structure
prompt: Predict potential side effects for: {compound}. First resolve and standardize the structure (canonical SMILES, sanitize) and confirm identity. (1) Query known side effects of structurally similar drugs (e.g., SIDER), citing sources. (2) Predict off-target activity against safety panels, naming tools/versions. (3) Analyze structural alerts for reactive metabolites. (4) Predict organ-specific toxicity (liver, kidney, heart, CNS). (5) Compare the risk profile with same-class drugs. (6) Compile a side-effect risk report with per-endpoint confidence scores. Treat all predictions as in silico hypotheses with uncertainty, not clinical evidence; flag out-of-domain cases and recommend experimental/clinical confirmation. Set a seed and record versions. If the compound identity is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a risk table as CSV (endpoint, prediction, confidence, basis), a risk dashboard as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize the highest-risk effects and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Formulation Optimization
description: Optimize drug formulation for bioavailability
prompt: Optimize the formulation of {compound} for {route} administration. First resolve and standardize the structure (canonical SMILES) and gather/estimate key inputs. (1) Analyze physicochemical properties (solubility, stability, LogP, pKa), naming prediction tools/versions and treating computed values as estimates with uncertainty. (2) Evaluate BCS classification (stating the permeability assumption). (3) Screen formulation strategies appropriate to {route} (salt form, amorphous solid dispersion, nanoparticles, cyclodextrins, lipid systems). (4) Predict dissolution profiles, noting model limitations. (5) Recommend excipient selection with compatibility rationale. (6) Design a stability-testing plan (ICH conditions). Treat recommendations as hypotheses requiring experimental confirmation; set seeds where applicable. If the compound or route is ambiguous and would change the strategy, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool; otherwise state assumptions. Deliverables: save a strategy-comparison table as CSV (approach, rationale, predicted benefit, risk), property/dissolution plots as PNG, and a Markdown report. Summarize the recommended formulation and caveats in chat and name the files.

name: Quantum Circuit Design
description: Design and simulate quantum circuits for specific algorithms
prompt: Design a quantum circuit for: {algorithm}. (1) State the target unitary or problem Hamiltonian, qubit count, and any assumptions; if the problem instance, register size, or success criterion is missing and would change the design, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Decompose into a native universal gate set and reduce circuit depth and two-qubit gate count via transpilation. (3) Simulate on a noiseless statevector backend; verify against the analytic ideal (e.g. exact eigenvalue, known marked state, or QFT phase) and report fidelity. (4) Analyze the output state or measurement distribution with shot-noise error bars. (5) Estimate hardware resources (qubits, depth, T-count) and likely error under a simple noise model. Set the seed and record qiskit/cirq versions. Save the circuit diagram and result histogram as PNG, metrics as CSV, and a Markdown summary; name the files and note validity limits.

placeholders.label: Algorithm or problem (e.g., VQE, Grover's, QFT, QAOA)

name: Quantum System Simulation
description: Simulate quantum many-body systems
prompt: Simulate the quantum many-body system: {system}. (1) Write the Hamiltonian explicitly with units and parameters; if lattice size, boundary conditions, or coupling values are missing and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions and note the Hilbert-space dimension. (2) Choose a method matched to size (exact diagonalization for small N, tensor networks/DMRG or quantum Monte Carlo for large N) and justify it. (3) Compute the ground-state energy and wavefunction; validate against an analytic limit or known benchmark and check convergence in basis/bond dimension. (4) Compute observables: {observables}, with error estimates. (5) Analyze phase transitions if relevant via order parameters and finite-size scaling. (6) Visualize the energy spectrum and correlation functions. Set seeds, record qutip/sympy versions. Save plots as PNG, numerical results as CSV, and a Markdown write-up; name the files and note validity limits.

placeholders.label: Quantum system (e.g., Ising model, Hubbard, spin chain)

name: Quantum Machine Learning
description: Build hybrid quantum-classical ML models
prompt: Build a hybrid quantum-classical ML model for: {task}. (1) State the dataset, feature dimension, and metric; if the data source or target is ambiguous and would change the design, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Encode data into quantum states (amplitude or angle encoding) and justify the qubit budget. (3) Design a parameterized ansatz; train via a hybrid optimization loop (gradient or parameter-shift), with a fixed train/test split. (4) Compare against a classical ML baseline on the same split using identical metrics. (5) Quantify expressibility and entanglement (e.g. entangling capability, Meyer-Wallach measure) and check trainability (barren-plateau gradient variance). (6) Assess practical quantum advantage honestly, noting where the classical baseline wins. Set seeds, record pennylane/qiskit/scikit-learn versions. Save loss curves and metric plots as PNG, results as CSV, and a Markdown summary; name the files and note validity limits.

name: Classical Mechanics Simulation
description: Simulate Newtonian or Lagrangian mechanical systems
prompt: Simulate the mechanical system: {system}. (1) Derive the equations of motion (Newtonian or Lagrangian), stating coordinates, parameters, and units; check dimensional consistency. If masses, lengths, or other parameters are missing and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Set initial conditions: {conditions}. (3) Integrate with an appropriate method (RK4 for general systems, symplectic Verlet for energy conservation); perform a step-size sensitivity check and report integration tolerance. (4) Visualize the trajectory and phase-space portrait. (5) Compute conserved quantities (energy, momentum, angular momentum) and report their drift as a numerical-accuracy check. (6) Analyze stability and sensitivity to initial conditions (e.g. Lyapunov estimate for chaotic systems). Set the seed, record numpy/scipy/sympy versions. Save plots as PNG, time series as CSV, and a Markdown write-up; name the files and note validity limits.

placeholders.label: Mechanical system (e.g., double pendulum, n-body, spring-mass)

name: Electromagnetic Simulation
description: Solve Maxwell's equations for EM field problems
prompt: Simulate the electromagnetic system: {system}. (1) Define geometry, materials (permittivity, permeability, conductivity), excitation, and boundary conditions with units; state the frequency range and key assumptions. If geometry, materials, or frequency are missing and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Choose a method matched to the problem (FDTD for broadband/transient, FEM for complex geometry, MoM for radiation) and justify it. (3) Solve for the electric and magnetic fields; verify mesh/grid convergence and check stability (e.g. CFL for FDTD). (4) Compute derived quantities (impedance, S-parameters, radiation pattern) and validate against an analytic case or benchmark. (5) Visualize field distributions. (6) Analyze resonances and mode profiles. Record solver/package versions. Save field plots as PNG, spectra/parameters as CSV, and a Markdown summary; name the files and note validity limits.

placeholders.label: EM system (antenna, waveguide, photonic crystal, etc.)

name: Statistical Mechanics Simulation
description: Monte Carlo and molecular dynamics for statistical ensembles
prompt: Perform a statistical mechanics simulation of: {system}. (1) Write the model Hamiltonian with units and parameters; state lattice size/particle count and boundary conditions. If system size or interaction parameters are missing and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Implement Monte Carlo (Metropolis, with detailed balance) or molecular dynamics in the appropriate ensemble. (3) Equilibrate then sample the canonical/microcanonical ensemble at T = {temperature}, discarding burn-in and accounting for autocorrelation time. (4) Compute thermodynamic averages (energy, heat capacity, magnetization) with error bars from block averaging. (5) Analyze phase transitions and critical exponents via finite-size scaling, validating against known results (e.g. exact 2D Ising Tc). (6) Generate a phase diagram if varying parameters. Set seeds, record package versions. Save plots as PNG, observables as CSV, and a Markdown write-up; name the files and note validity limits.

placeholders.label: System (e.g., Ising model, Lennard-Jones fluid, XY model)

name: Particle Physics Data Analysis
description: Analyze high-energy physics collision data
prompt: Analyze the uploaded particle physics data. (1) Inspect the file and apply event-selection cuts for {channel}, documenting each cut and its efficiency. (2) Reconstruct invariant-mass distributions with appropriate binning. (3) Fit signal-plus-background models (state the functional forms) by maximum likelihood, reporting parameter uncertainties and goodness of fit. (4) Calculate signal significance (e.g. profile-likelihood or S/sqrt(B)) with systematic and statistical contributions. (5) Estimate cross-section or branching ratio with propagated uncertainties. (6) Compare with Standard Model predictions and note tension if any. (7) Generate publication-quality plots with statistical uncertainty bands. If the channel selection or expected background is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. Set the seed, record package versions. Save plots as PNG, fit results as CSV, and a Markdown summary; name the files and note validity limits.

placeholders.label: Decay channel or process (e.g., H→γγ, Z→μμ)

name: Band Structure Calculation
description: Compute electronic band structures and density of states
prompt: Calculate the electronic band structure for: {material}. (1) Set up the crystal structure and lattice parameters with units, citing the source structure; state assumptions. If the structure or phase is ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Choose a method matched to accuracy/cost (DFT with a stated functional, or tight-binding); justify k-mesh and basis/cutoff and check convergence. (3) Compute bands along high-symmetry paths in the Brillouin zone. (4) Calculate the total and projected density of states. (5) Identify the band gap (direct/indirect), Fermi level, and orbital character, noting that semilocal DFT typically underestimates gaps. (6) Visualize the band structure and projected DOS. Record package/functional versions. Save plots as PNG, bands/DOS data as CSV, and a Markdown write-up; name the files and note validity limits.

name: Optics Simulation
description: Simulate optical systems: lenses, lasers, waveguides
prompt: Simulate the optical system: {system}. (1) Define the configuration, components, wavelength, and units; state whether ray or wave optics applies and why. If aperture, wavelength, or component specs are missing and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Perform ray tracing or wave-optics (Fresnel/Fraunhofer) propagation with adequate sampling; check grid/aperture sampling convergence. (3) Compute key metrics: {metrics}. (4) Analyze aberrations, diffraction, or interference; sanity-check against analytic limits (e.g. diffraction-limited spot size, thin-lens equation). (5) Optimize system parameters toward a stated target. (6) Visualize beam profiles and optical fields. Record package versions. Save plots as PNG, metric tables as CSV, and a Markdown summary; name the files and note validity limits.

placeholders.label: Metrics to compute (e.g., PSF, MTF, beam waist, NA)

name: Thermodynamic Modeling
description: Model thermodynamic cycles and energy systems
prompt: Model the thermodynamic system: {system}. (1) Define the working fluid, the cycle, and all state points; state assumptions (steady state, component efficiencies) and units. If pressures, temperatures, or efficiencies are missing and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Calculate state properties at each point (T, P, h, s, v) using a property library or equation of state, checking dimensional consistency. (3) Compute cycle efficiency and net work output; report all energy balances and verify the first and second laws (no entropy decrease). (4) Generate T-s and P-v diagrams. (5) Perform sensitivity analysis on key parameters (e.g. boiler pressure, pinch point). (6) Compare with the ideal (Carnot/ideal-cycle) limit. Record package versions. Save diagrams as PNG, state-point and balance tables as CSV, and a Markdown write-up; name the files and note validity limits.

placeholders.label: Thermodynamic system (Rankine, Brayton, refrigeration, heat pump)

name: Gravitational Wave Analysis
description: Analyze LIGO/Virgo gravitational wave signal data
prompt: Analyze the uploaded gravitational-wave strain data. (1) Inspect sampling rate and duration; apply bandpass filtering and noise whitening using an estimated PSD. (2) Compute spectrograms and Q-transforms to visualize candidate signals. (3) Perform matched filtering against {templates}, reporting the matched-filter SNR time series. (4) Estimate source parameters (chirp mass, spin, distance) with credible intervals via the chosen inference method. (5) Calculate the signal-to-noise ratio and assess significance against the noise background. (6) Generate whitened strain and spectrogram visualizations. (7) Compare the recovered signal with the best-fit waveform template (residual check). If the detector/data conventions or template family are ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. Set seeds, record package versions. Save plots as PNG, parameter estimates as CSV, and a Markdown summary; name the files and note validity limits.

name: Plasma Physics Simulation
description: Simulate plasma behavior and magnetohydrodynamics
prompt: Simulate the plasma system: {system}. (1) Define plasma parameters (density, temperature, magnetic field) with units; compute key dimensionless numbers (e.g. beta, Debye length, gyroradius) to justify the regime. If configuration parameters or boundary conditions are missing and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Choose a model matched to scales (ideal/resistive MHD, PIC, or Vlasov) and justify it. (3) Set boundary and initial conditions. (4) Evolve the system with a stable scheme (respect CFL), tracking: {quantities}, and monitor conserved quantities (energy, mass, divergence-free B) as accuracy checks. (5) Analyze instabilities and wave modes, comparing growth rates to linear theory where possible. (6) Visualize density, field, and velocity distributions. Set seeds, record package versions. Save plots as PNG, time series as CSV, and a Markdown write-up; name the files and note validity limits.

name: Quantum Error Correction
description: Design and analyze quantum error-correcting codes
prompt: Design a quantum error-correction scheme for: {noise_model}. (1) State the noise channel, its error rate, and assumptions (i.i.d. per-gate, measurement error); if the rate or qubit budget is missing and would change conclusions, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Select an appropriate QEC code (surface code, Steane, Shor) and justify the choice for this noise. (3) Implement encoding and syndrome-measurement circuits with a stated decoder. (4) Simulate logical error rate vs physical error rate over many shots, reporting Monte Carlo error bars. (5) Estimate the error threshold and plot the threshold diagram (crossing of curves for several code distances). (6) Analyze resource overhead (physical qubits per logical qubit) and compare with an alternative code. Set seeds, record qiskit/cirq versions. Save plots as PNG, error-rate data as CSV, and a Markdown summary; name the files and note validity limits.

name: Nuclear Decay Analysis
description: Analyze radioactive decay data and nuclear reactions
prompt: Analyze the nuclear decay data for: {isotope}. (1) Fit exponential (or multi-component Bateman) decay curves by weighted least squares to determine the half-life, reporting parameter uncertainties and goodness of fit; account for Poisson counting statistics. (2) Identify decay products and branching ratios. (3) Construct the decay chain. (4) Calculate specific activity and dose rates with units and propagated uncertainty. (5) Compare half-lives and branching ratios with literature/nuclear-database values (e.g. ENSDF) and note discrepancies. (6) Generate a decay-scheme diagram. If the data format or measurement geometry is ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. Set the seed, record package versions. Save plots as PNG, fitted parameters and activities as CSV, and a Markdown write-up; name the files and note validity limits.

name: Crystal Structure Prediction
description: Predict stable crystal structures for a composition
prompt: Predict stable crystal structures for: {composition}. (1) Search existing entries in materials databases (Materials Project, ICSD) and record provenance; state assumptions. If oxidation states or target conditions (pressure, temperature) are ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Generate candidate structures via ion substitution and known prototypes. (3) Optimize each with DFT (state functional and convergence criteria). (4) Evaluate formation energies and thermodynamic stability, propagating method-level uncertainty. (5) Construct the convex hull and report energy-above-hull for each candidate. (6) Identify the ground state and low-energy polymorphs; report space groups and lattice parameters. Note that semilocal DFT energies carry systematic error. Record pymatgen/functional versions. Save the hull plot and structures as PNG/structure files, energies as CSV, and a Markdown summary; name the files and note validity limits.

name: Phase Diagram Analysis
description: Construct and interpret phase diagrams
prompt: Construct a phase diagram for the {system} system. (1) Collect thermodynamic data from literature and databases (e.g. CALPHAD assessments), recording sources and units. If the composition range, pressure, or relevant phases are ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Calculate phase boundaries by minimizing Gibbs energy with stated solution models. (3) Identify single-phase and multi-phase regions. (4) Mark invariant reactions (eutectic, peritectic, eutectoid) with their temperatures and compositions. (5) Overlay experimental data if provided and quantify agreement. (6) Visualize the complete labeled phase diagram, noting model validity limits. Record pymatgen/package versions. Save the diagram as PNG, boundary/invariant-point data as CSV, and a Markdown write-up; name the files.

name: Mechanical Property Prediction
description: Predict elastic moduli, hardness, and strength of materials
prompt: Predict the mechanical properties of: {material}. (1) State the structure and method (DFT strain-stress or empirical potential), units, and assumptions; if the structure or phase is ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Calculate the elastic-constant tensor (C_ij) and verify Born mechanical-stability criteria as a sanity check. (3) Derive bulk, shear, and Young's moduli (Voigt-Reuss-Hill averages). (4) Estimate Vickers hardness via an established empirical model. (5) Analyze elastic anisotropy (Zener ratio, universal anisotropy index). (6) Assess Pugh's ratio (ductile vs brittle) and compare with known materials of similar composition. Note method-level error. Record pymatgen/package versions. Save anisotropy/property plots as PNG, the C_ij tensor and derived moduli as CSV, and a Markdown summary; name the files and note validity limits.

name: High-Throughput Materials Screening
description: Screen materials databases for target properties
prompt: Screen materials for: {application}. Target properties: {properties}. (1) Query materials databases (Materials Project, AFLOW, OQMD), recording query parameters and provenance; if the property ranges or constraints are ambiguous and would change the shortlist, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Filter by target property ranges with explicit units and thresholds. (3) Rank candidates by a transparent multi-objective score (state weights or Pareto criterion). (4) Check stability (formation energy, energy-above-hull) and flag method uncertainty. (5) Assess synthesizability from literature and known precursors. (6) Report the top 20 candidates with all relevant properties. Record pymatgen/package versions. Save a ranking plot as PNG, the full candidate table as CSV, and a Markdown summary; name the files and note validity limits and database coverage gaps.

placeholders.label: Target application (e.g., battery cathode, thermoelectric, photocatalyst)

name: Defect Analysis
description: Analyze point defects, vacancies, and impurities in crystals
prompt: Analyze defects in: {material}. (1) State the host structure, method (DFT with supercell and stated functional), and units; if the relevant chemical environment, charge states, or growth conditions are ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Calculate formation energies for common point defects (vacancies, interstitials, antisites), applying finite-size and charged-defect corrections. (3) Determine charge-state transition levels in the gap. (4) Compute equilibrium defect concentrations at {temperature} using Boltzmann statistics. (5) Analyze migration barriers (e.g. NEB) for key defects. (6) Predict the dominant defect type as a function of chemical potential and Fermi level. Note semilocal-DFT gap error. Record pymatgen/package versions. Save formation-energy and transition-level plots as PNG, energies as CSV, and a Markdown write-up discussing property implications; name the files and note validity limits.

name: Alloy Design
description: Design alloys with targeted mechanical or functional properties
prompt: Design an alloy for: {application}. Requirements: {requirements}. (1) Propose candidate composition ranges from CALPHAD and literature, stating units and assumptions; if the requirements lack quantitative targets or service conditions that would change the design, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Predict phase stability and expected microstructure as a function of composition and processing temperature. (3) Estimate mechanical properties (yield strength, hardness, ductility) via established models, reporting expected uncertainty. (4) Assess corrosion resistance. (5) Check processability (castability, weldability, segregation tendency). (6) Recommend the top 3 compositions for experimental validation, with rationale. Record package/database versions. Save a property-vs-composition plot as PNG, the candidate comparison table as CSV, and a Markdown design report; name the files and note model validity limits.

name: Battery Material Analysis
description: Evaluate materials for electrochemical energy storage
prompt: Evaluate {material} as a {component} for batteries. (1) State the working ion, method, and units; if the operating voltage window or counter-electrode is ambiguous and would change conclusions, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Calculate the voltage profile and theoretical gravimetric/volumetric capacity from formation energies of the relevant phases. (3) Analyze structural stability during (de)intercalation (volume change, phase transitions). (4) Compute ion-migration barriers (e.g. NEB) and estimate diffusivity. (5) Assess electronic conductivity (band gap / DOS at the Fermi level). (6) Compare with state-of-the-art materials and identify likely degradation mechanisms. Report all relevant electrochemical properties with units and method uncertainty. Record pymatgen/package versions. Save voltage-profile and migration plots as PNG, properties as CSV, and a Markdown summary; name the files and note validity limits.

placeholders.label: Battery component (cathode, anode, electrolyte, solid electrolyte)

name: Thermal Conductivity Modeling
description: Predict thermal transport properties of materials
prompt: Model the thermal conductivity of: {material}. (1) State the structure, method (e.g. DFT phonons via finite displacements or DFPT), and units; if the structure or relevant scattering processes are ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Calculate phonon dispersion relations and check for stability (no large imaginary modes) as a sanity check; verify supercell/q-mesh convergence. (3) Compute the phonon density of states. (4) Estimate lattice thermal conductivity by solving the Boltzmann transport equation (including anharmonic three-phonon scattering). (5) Analyze dominant phonon scattering mechanisms. (6) Predict the temperature dependence from {t_min} to {t_max} K and compare with experimental data if available, noting method uncertainty. Record package versions. Save dispersion and kappa(T) plots as PNG, data as CSV, and a Markdown write-up; name the files and note validity limits.

name: Surface Energy Analysis
description: Calculate surface energies and predict crystal morphology
prompt: Calculate surface energies for: {material}. (1) State the bulk structure, method (DFT with stated functional), and units; if the relevant facets or environment are ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Identify low-index surfaces. (3) Construct symmetric surface slab models and verify convergence with respect to slab thickness and vacuum spacing. (4) Calculate the surface energy of each facet, using consistent bulk references. (5) Construct the Wulff shape (equilibrium crystal morphology). (6) Analyze surface terminations and reconstructions, and predict adsorption energies for {adsorbate} if specified, noting coverage assumptions. Record pymatgen/package versions. Save the Wulff shape and surface-energy plots as PNG, energies as CSV, and a Markdown summary; name the files and note validity limits.

name: Polymer Property Prediction
description: Predict thermal, mechanical, and transport properties of polymers
prompt: Predict properties for the polymer: {polymer}. (1) Parse the repeat unit and state the method (group-contribution, QSPR/ML, or simulation) and units; if the molecular weight, tacticity, or processing state is ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Calculate the glass-transition temperature (Tg). (3) Predict the thermal decomposition temperature. (4) Estimate mechanical properties (modulus, tensile strength). (5) Predict gas permeability (O2, N2, CO2). (6) Assess solubility parameters (e.g. Hansen/Hildebrand). For each property, report the model's expected error and compare with experimental data and similar polymers in the literature. Set the seed, record rdkit/deepchem versions. Save a property comparison plot as PNG, the property table as CSV, and a Markdown summary; name the files and note validity limits.

name: Electronic Band Structure
description: Compute electronic band structure and DOS for materials
prompt: Calculate the electronic band structure for: {material}. (1) State the structure source, method (DFT with a named functional), and units; if the structure or magnetic state is ambiguous and would change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool, else state assumptions. (2) Optimize the crystal structure and verify force/stress convergence. (3) Compute bands along the standard high-symmetry k-path, checking k-mesh and energy-cutoff convergence. (4) Calculate the total and projected density of states. (5) Determine the band gap (direct/indirect), noting that semilocal DFT underestimates gaps and suggesting a correction if accuracy matters. (6) Analyze orbital character of bands near the Fermi level and compute effective masses if semiconducting. Record pymatgen/functional versions. Save band and PDOS plots as PNG, the data as CSV, and a Markdown write-up; name the files and note validity limits.

name: Materials Science Literature Review
description: Survey literature for a materials science topic
prompt: Conduct a comprehensive literature review on: {topic} in materials science. (1) Search peer-reviewed publications from the last {years} years, recording databases, queries, and dates for reproducibility. (2) Identify key synthesis methods and characterization techniques. (3) Compile reported property values in a comparison table with units, measurement conditions, and citations. (4) Identify trends and quantify discrepancies between studies, distinguishing measurement scatter from genuine disagreement. (5) Highlight open challenges and unresolved questions. (6) Suggest promising, specific research directions. Note source quality and any coverage gaps. Save the comparison table as CSV, any trend plots as PNG, and the full review with citations as Markdown; name the files and summarize key findings in chat.

name: Clinical Trial Analysis
description: Analyze clinical trial data with appropriate endpoints
prompt: Analyze the uploaded clinical trial data. Pre-specify the primary endpoint and analysis before looking at comparisons; if the population, randomization, or comparator arm is ambiguous and would materially change results, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first, otherwise state your assumptions. (1) Describe the study population: demographics and baseline characteristics with a CONSORT participant flow. (2) Analyze the primary endpoint {endpoint} with effect sizes and 95% confidence intervals, reporting both absolute and relative measures. (3) Run intention-to-treat (primary) and per-protocol analyses, accounting for missing data. (4) Generate Kaplan-Meier curves for time-to-event endpoints. (5) Run pre-specified subgroup analyses with interaction tests, correcting for multiple comparisons. (6) Tabulate adverse events. Follow CONSORT; set a random seed and record package versions. This is research and decision-support only, not clinical advice. Save KM curves and forest plots as PNG, result tables as CSV, and a findings report as Markdown; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations.

name: Clinical Survival Analysis
description: Kaplan-Meier, Cox regression for clinical outcomes
prompt: Perform survival analysis on the uploaded clinical data. Outcome: {outcome}, follow-up time in {time_unit}. If the event definition, censoring rule, or comparison would materially change results and is ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions. (1) Kaplan-Meier estimates for overall and subgroup survival with 95% CIs and numbers-at-risk. (2) Log-rank tests comparing {groups}. (3) Multivariable Cox regression adjusting for {covariates}, reporting hazard ratios with 95% CIs. (4) Check the proportional-hazards assumption (Schoenfeld residuals) and handle violations. (5) Generate a forest plot of hazard ratios. (6) Report median survival and 5-year rates with CIs. Set a random seed, record package versions (lifelines/scikit-survival), and address missing data. Research and decision-support only, not clinical advice. Save KM curves and the forest plot as PNG, tables as CSV, and a concise Markdown report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations.

name: Epidemiological Study Design
description: Design and analyze observational epidemiological studies
prompt: Design an epidemiological study to investigate: {question}. Study type: {study_type}. If the exposure, outcome, or target population is ambiguous in a way that changes the design, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions. (1) Define the study population, source, and sampling strategy. (2) Specify precise exposure and outcome definitions with measurement and validity. (3) Identify confounders and effect modifiers and a strategy to control them (a DAG helps). (4) Calculate required sample size and power, stating assumptions. (5) Plan the statistical analysis with appropriate adjusted measures (OR, RR, hazard ratios) reported with 95% CIs, plus absolute measures, and correction for multiple comparisons. (6) Describe methods to address selection, information, and confounding bias, and missing-data handling. Follow STROBE for any writeup; pre-specify endpoints. This is research planning, not clinical advice. Save the protocol and analysis plan as Markdown plus any tables as CSV; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations.

name: Medical Image Analysis
description: Analyze radiological or pathological images
prompt: Analyze the uploaded medical images ({modality}). If the clinical question or expected output for {target_structure} is ambiguous in a way that changes the analysis, ask one clarifying question via the interview tool first; otherwise state assumptions. (1) Preprocess: normalize intensities, correct artifacts, and document spacing/orientation from DICOM metadata. (2) Apply segmentation for {target_structure} and report segmentation quality. (3) Extract quantitative features (shape, texture, intensity) with reproducible settings. (4) If applicable, classify findings with an ML model, reporting performance with confidence intervals and guarding against data leakage. (5) Generate annotated output images with overlays. (6) Report findings in standard radiological/pathological format. Set a random seed and record package versions (pydicom and others). These are research outputs and decision-support only, not a diagnosis or substitute for a qualified reader. Save annotated images and figures as PNG, feature/result tables as CSV, and a concise Markdown report; summarize in chat, name the files, and note limitations.
